name: forkandfloat-recipes
description: Fork & Float recipe hub — auto-generated from Google Form submissions

scripts.test:
node generate-recipe.js '{"title":"Shirazi Salad","description":"Crisp cucumber, ripe tomato, red onion, and dried lime. A Persian classic.","category":"Salads · Vegetarian","prepTime":"10 min","cookTime":"None","servings":"4","difficulty":"Easy","tags":["salads","vegetarian","quick"],"emoji":"🥗","ingredients":"Persian cucumbers [3, finely diced]
Ripe tomatoes [2, finely diced]
Red onion [½, finely diced]
Fresh parsley [small handful]
Dried lime powder [1 tsp]
Lemon juice [2 tbsp]
Olive oil [2 tbsp]
Flaky sea salt [to taste]","steps":"Dice everything small
Cut cucumbers, tomatoes, and red onion into a very fine, even dice — about 5mm. The smaller and more uniform the cut, the better the salad eats.

Make the dressing
Whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, and dried lime powder. Dried lime is what makes this distinctly Persian — find it at any Middle Eastern grocery.

Combine and rest
Toss everything together, season generously with flaky sea salt, and let it sit for 5–10 minutes before serving.","howToServe":"Alongside saffron rice and any grilled protein
As part of a mezze spread with flatbread
Spooned over whipped feta as a starter
Best eaten fresh — does not keep once dressed","chefsNote":"My mum made this for every single family dinner. The dried lime is non-negotiable — it gives it that unmistakable Persian tang that fresh lemon alone cannot replicate."}'

author: Fork & Float
